originalAlarm.description: Value stored to 'p' is never read

reasoning: 061fd0a353e.c L16: 'p = *argv++, --argc' dead store. p is declared (L7) and conditionally assigned in 'case W' (L16, when r!=0). After break, no subsequent code reads p (the switch ends and main returns). The dead store occurs in ALL configurations (case 'W' is not inside any #ifdef). However, the PC only covers 3 of 4 configurations: it misses CONFIG_FEATURE_HDPARM_HDIO_UNREGISTER_HWIF=T AND CONFIG_FEATURE_HDPARM_HDIO_SCAN_HWIF=F, where the dead store still exists. PC is under-constrained (too narrow). New bug.
